title: 1.1: Extracts CLI flag-related logic from getRuntimeOptions into a separate reusable helper

annotations.description: Verifies that CLI flag reading logic is moved into its own helper function that can be called and tested independently from environment variable processing.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 50-59: creates runtimeOptionsFromFlags function that extracts all flag reading (getNullString/getNullBool calls) into a dedicated helper returning RuntimeOptions.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states getRuntimeOptions is 'doing all the flags' and requests to 'refactor into reusable and single purpose helpers'. [3: Logical Necessity] Separating flag extraction into its own helper achieves single-purpose design and enables isolated testing. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must extract CLI flag-related logic into a separate reusable helper.

title: 1.2: Extracts environment variable processing logic from getRuntimeOptions into a separate reusable helper

annotations.description: Verifies that environment variable reading and processing logic is moved into its own helper function that can be called and tested independently from CLI flag processing.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 63-130: creates populateRuntimeOptionsFromEnv function that extracts all K6_* environment variable processing into a dedicated helper.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states getRuntimeOptions handles 'env vars' and requests 'single purpose helpers'. [3: Logical Necessity] Separating environment processing into its own helper achieves single-purpose design and enables isolated testing. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must extract environment variable processing logic into a separate reusable helper.

title: 1.3: Refactors getRuntimeOptions to delegate to the extracted helpers

annotations.description: Verifies that getRuntimeOptions composes the new flag-extraction and environment-population helpers rather than retaining the original inline logic. The function should delegate to the extracted helpers and only keep orchestration concerns (such as the --env flag loop) itself.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 13-45: getRuntimeOptions calls populateRuntimeOptionsFromEnv(runtimeOptionsFromFlags(flags), environment) to delegate flag reading and env-var processing, retaining only the --env key-value loop and userEnvVarName validation inline.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requests to 'refactor into reusable and single purpose helpers', which implies getRuntimeOptions must actually use them rather than keeping inline logic alongside dead helper functions. [3: Logical Necessity] Creating helpers without wiring them into getRuntimeOptions would leave the original monolithic logic unchanged, defeating the purpose of the refactor. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must have getRuntimeOptions delegate to the extracted helpers.

title: 4.1: Removes or alters existing runtime option handling (such as dropping support for K6_COMPATIBILITY_MODE or changing how SSLKEYLOGFILE is processed)

annotations.description: Detects if any existing runtime option handling is removed or functionally altered. All environment variables and CLI flags must remain functional after refactoring.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 63-130 preserve all environment variable checks; lines 50-59 preserve all flag extractions; no option handling is removed.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The existing codebase has comprehensive test coverage in runtime_options_test.go that validates all option handling. [3: Logical Necessity] Removing option handling would break existing functionality and fail tests. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY solution that removes or alters existing runtime option handling triggers this negative criterion.

title: 4.2: Changes the function signature of getRuntimeOptions (such as altering parameters, return type, or renaming the function)

annotations.description: Detects if the public signature of getRuntimeOptions is changed. The function must continue to accept (flags *pflag.FlagSet, environment map[string]string) and return (lib.RuntimeOptions, error).
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 13-16: getRuntimeOptions retains exact signature (flags *pflag.FlagSet, environment map[string]string) (lib.RuntimeOptions, error).
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The function is called from internal/cmd/test_load.go and tests in runtime_options_test.go with specific argument types. [3: Logical Necessity] Changing the signature would cause compilation failures in callers. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY solution that changes the function signature of getRuntimeOptions triggers this negative criterion.

title: 4.3: Reverses CLI flag precedence over environment variables

annotations.description: Detects if the refactored code changes the existing behavior where CLI flags take priority over environment variables. Environment values should only apply when CLI flag was not explicitly set.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 66-73: precedence checks like '!opts.TestType.Valid && ok' ensure env vars only apply when CLI flag was not explicitly set.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The existing code uses null.Bool/null.String Valid field to track CLI-set values and only applies env vars when Valid is false. [3: Logical Necessity] Reversing precedence would alter existing behavior and break test cases in runtime_options_test.go. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY solution that reverses CLI flag precedence over environment variables triggers this negative criterion.
